segments.text: And God spoke all these words, saying:
I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall have no other gods before Me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall not make for yourself a carved image—any likeness of anything that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall not bow down to them nor serve them. For I, the Lord your God, am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children to the third and fourth generations of those who hate Me,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But showing mercy to thousands, to those who love Me and keep My commandments.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall not take the name of the Lord your God in vain, for the Lord will not hold him guiltless who takes His name in vain.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Six days you shall labor and do all your work,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But the seventh day is the Sabbath of the Lord your God. In it you shall do no work: you, nor your son, nor your daughter, nor your male servant, nor your female servant, nor your cattle, nor your stranger who is within your gates.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For in six days the Lord made the heavens and the earth, the sea, and all that is in them, and rested the seventh day. Therefore the Lord blessed the Sabbath day and hallowed it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long upon the land which the Lord your God is giving you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall not murder.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall not commit adultery.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall not steal.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall not covet your neighbor's house; you shall not covet your neighbor's wife, nor his male servant, nor his female servant, nor his ox, nor his donkey, nor anything that is your neighbor's.
segments.speaker: God

verses.title: The People Afraid of God's Presence

segments.text: Now all the people witnessed the thunderings, the lightning flashes, the sound of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking; and when the people saw it, they trembled and stood afar off.
Then they said to Moses,
You speak with us, and we will hear; but let not God speak with us, lest we die.
segments.speaker: The People

segments.text: And Moses said to the people,
Do not fear; for God has come to test you, and that His fear may be before you, so that you may not sin.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: So the people stood afar off, but Moses drew near the thick darkness where God was.
Then the Lord said to Moses,
Thus you shall say to the children of Israel: 'You have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall not make anything to be with Me—gods of silver or gods of gold you shall not make for yourselves.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: An altar of earth you shall make for Me, and you shall sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your oxen. In every place where I record My name I will come to you, and I will bless you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And if you make Me an altar of stone, you shall not build it of hewn stone; for if you use your tool on it, you have profaned it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Nor shall you go up by steps to My altar, that your nakedness may not be exposed on it.'
segments.speaker: God
